ORCH-001.title: Define agent registry schema and bootstrap
ORCH-001.description: AgentRegistry class: central catalog with CRUD, circuit breakers, capability lookup, cost-tier filtering, fallback chains, rolling latency metrics. Pre-registers 9 default agents.
ORCH-001.rationale: Every orchestration decision queries the registry. Nothing runs without it.
ORCH-001.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

ORCH-002.title: Implement intent classifier routing table
ORCH-002.description: Supervisor classifyIntent(): keyword-weighted matching against 10 intent patterns with entity extraction (locations, prices, property types, features). Confidence = matched chars / available, capped 0.99.
ORCH-002.rationale: Keyword matching for determinism, zero latency, zero cost, and testability over LLM classification.
ORCH-002.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

ORCH-003.title: Add circuit breaker with configurable thresholds
ORCH-003.description: CircuitBreaker: CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN state machine. Configurable: failureThreshold(3), resetTimeoutMs(60s), halfOpenMaxAttempts(1), monitorWindowMs(120s).
ORCH-003.rationale: Prevents cascading failures from broken upstream dependencies. HALF_OPEN enables automatic recovery.
ORCH-003.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

ORCH-004.title: Build dead-letter queue for failed agent tasks
ORCH-004.description: DeadLetterQueue captures tasks that exhausted all recovery. Entries: original input, errors, attempts, timestamps, replayable flag.
ORCH-004.rationale: Failed tasks that silently disappear create data loss. DLQ makes failures visible, auditable, recoverable.
ORCH-004.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test
acceptanceCriteria: stats() returns counts by error type

ORCH-005.title: Implement cost budget tracker per request
ORCH-005.description: CostBudgetManager: per-session/request/daily limits against MODEL_CONFIGS pricing. Alerts: none/warning(85%)/critical(95%)/exceeded. Model downgrade suggestions.
ORCH-005.rationale: Runaway agents can burn hundreds of dollars in minutes. Three granularities enable graceful degradation.
ORCH-005.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

ORCH-006.title: Add handoff depth guard and circular detection
ORCH-006.description: HandoffManager: max depth 5, circular detection via chain history. Supports delegation/escalation/fallback/specialization/review types.
ORCH-006.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

ORCH-007.title: Implement batch processing for multi-property queries
ORCH-007.description: BatchProcessor: parallel execution with configurable concurrency(3). Per-item status, result aggregation, progress, cancellation.
ORCH-007.rationale: Multi-property queries should parallelize with bounded concurrency to avoid overwhelming downstream.
ORCH-007.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

ORCH-008.title: Add graceful degradation fallback chain
ORCH-008.description: ErrorRecoveryEngine: maps errors to strategies (backoff/fallback/degrade/abort). Exponential backoff with jitter. Integrates with circuit breakers.
ORCH-008.rationale: One-size-fits-all retry wastes tokens. Error classification applies cheapest effective strategy per error type.
ORCH-008.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

CCFG-001.title: Create pre-command destructive guard hook
CCFG-001.description: DCG blocks: git reset --hard, git clean -fd, git checkout --, git push --force, rm -rf. Suggests safe alternatives.
CCFG-001.rationale: Instructions don't prevent execution. Mechanical enforcement required.

CCFG-002.title: Write run-agent-pipeline skill
CCFG-002.description: Skill for understand->plan->implement->test->review->security->document pipeline with task size classification.
CCFG-002.rationale: Ensures consistent quality gates across agents.

CCFG-003.title: Write create-mcp-server skill
CCFG-003.description: Skill for scaffolding MCP domain servers: entry, tools, Zod schemas, registry registration.

CCFG-004.title: Write debug-agent-issue skill
CCFG-004.description: Debugging skill: 7 failure modes with symptoms, diagnosis steps, and fix actions.
CCFG-004.rationale: Maps failure symptoms to diagnostics, preventing random debugging.

CCFG-005.title: Write add-agent skill
CCFG-005.description: Skill for adding agents: model tier, capabilities, system prompt, fallback chain, intent params, registry entry.
CCFG-005.rationale: Multi-file operation codified to prevent missed steps.

CCFG-006.title: Write compound-review skill
CCFG-006.description: Multi-pass review: self-review, cross-agent, random exploration, convergence detection.

PRMT-001.title: Define system prompt template structure
PRMT-001.description: XML-structured template: <role>,<capabilities>,<instructions>,<grounding_rules>,<intent_engineering>,<output_format>,<examples>. Parameterized for role-specific content.
PRMT-001.rationale: Consistent structure ensures predictable behavior and auditable prompts across all agents.

PRMT-002.title: Create grounding rules for property data
PRMT-002.description: 10 rules: no fabrication, cite sources, mark approximations, verify availability, distinguish facts/inference, consistent units, data freshness, cross-reference, flag conflicts, refuse vs hallucinate. GroundingValidator detects violations.
PRMT-002.rationale: Real estate data has legal/financial implications. Two-layer defense: prevention + detection.
PRMT-002.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

PRMT-003.title: Build prompt versioning system
PRMT-003.description: PromptRegistry: version tracking, agent-to-version mapping, usage metrics per version, rollback support.
PRMT-003.rationale: Silent prompt degradation requires versioning for traceability.

PRMT-004.title: Add structured output schemas for agent responses
PRMT-004.description: Zod schemas: IntentClassification, PropertySearchResponse, MarketAnalysisOutput, RecommendationOutput.
PRMT-004.rationale: Unstructured output is unparseable. Zod provides runtime + compile-time types from single source.
PRMT-004.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

PRMT-005.title: Implement prompt cache strategy
PRMT-005.description: 6-layer caching: System(cached), Static(cached), RAG(cached), History(cached w/ boundary), Recent(uncached), Task(uncached).
PRMT-005.rationale: Reduces costs 80%+ by caching stable prefix.
PRMT-005.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

PRMT-006.title: Add grounding validator for fabricated data
PRMT-006.description: Extended validator: unsourced prices, fabricated addresses, unsupported percentages, date claims without freshness.
PRMT-006.rationale: Rules tell agents what not to do. Validator catches when they do it anyway.
PRMT-006.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

PRMT-007.title: Create agent-specific system prompts for all roles
PRMT-007.description: 7 role-specific prompts: supervisor, property-search, market-analyst, data-enrichment, recommendation, conversation-manager, quality-reviewer.
PRMT-007.rationale: Role-specific prompts constrain agents to their domain and prevent scope creep.

PRMT-008.title: Add XML tag structure to system prompts
PRMT-008.description: Consistent XML nesting across all 7 prompts: <system_prompt>><role>+<capabilities>+<instructions>+<grounding_rules>+<output_format>+<examples>.
PRMT-008.rationale: XML tags create unambiguous section boundaries. Consistent structure enables automated auditing.

MCP-001.title: Add auth token validation to MCP servers
MCP-001.description: HMAC-based scoped auth tokens: agent ID, tool scopes, expiry, signature. Validation at server entry.
MCP-001.rationale: Without auth, any process calls any tool. Scoped tokens enforce agent-to-tool ACLs.

MCP-002.title: Add rate limiting middleware per MCP server
MCP-002.description: Sliding window rate limiting per agent and per tool. 429 with retry-after. In-memory state.
MCP-002.rationale: Runaway loops can fire thousands of calls/minute. Per-agent limiting bounds blast radius.

MCP-003.title: Implement shared MCP config loader
MCP-003.description: Centralized config from env vars and config files. Typed per domain server. Validates required, provides defaults.
MCP-003.rationale: Prevents duplicate parsing, ensures consistent defaults.

MCP-004.title: Create property-db MCP server scaffold
MCP-004.description: Property MCP: search, detail, ZPID resolution, comparison. Zod input, text-first output.
MCP-004.rationale: Core domain needs clean validated tool interface.

MCP-005.title: Create vector-search MCP server scaffold
MCP-005.description: Semantic search against Pinecone: NL query, metadata filter, score-based ranking.
MCP-005.rationale: Enables natural-language property queries keyword search cannot handle.

MCP-006.title: Create graph-query MCP server scaffold
MCP-006.description: Neo4j graph traversal: related properties, neighborhoods, property-amenity-school relationships, graph analytics.
MCP-006.rationale: Graph captures connections relational queries miss.

MCP-007.title: Create geocoding MCP server scaffold
MCP-007.description: Geocoding/commute MCP: address geocoding, reverse geocoding, commute time, distance.
MCP-007.rationale: Location central to real estate. Clean abstraction over geocoding APIs.

MCP-008.title: Create market-data MCP server scaffold
MCP-008.description: Market MCP: trends, price history, inventory, days-on-market, comps. Aggregates multiple backends.
MCP-008.rationale: Powers market-analyst with normalized data interface.

CTX-001.title: Implement token budget allocator
CTX-001.description: Dynamic allocation: system(2k), static(1k), RAG(4k), conversation(3k+500/10msgs), tool(5k), generation(4k). Total 128k. Compaction at 85%.
CTX-001.rationale: Without budget, agents stuff context until overflow.
CTX-001.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

CTX-002.title: Build multi-level context cache (L1/L2)
CTX-002.description: L1: in-memory LRU for hot data. L2: persistent for warm data. TTL, access tracking, size accounting.
CTX-002.rationale: Same properties queried repeatedly. L1=sub-ms. L2=persists across turns.
CTX-002.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

CTX-003.title: Create conversation store with sliding window
CTX-003.description: Recent N messages full fidelity, older summarized within budget. Add/getRecent/getAll/summarize/clear.
CTX-003.rationale: Long conversations exceed context windows. Window keeps recent detail.
CTX-003.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

CTX-004.title: Implement coherence manager for context merging
CTX-004.description: Merges conversation/RAG/tools/entities. Resolves conflicts, deduplicates, ensures consistency.
CTX-004.rationale: Multiple sources may contradict. Recency and source-trust heuristics resolve.

CTX-005.title: Add context assembly strategies
CTX-005.description: 6 strategies: slidingWindow, summarizeRecent, entityAnchored, ragFirst, hierarchical + getStrategyForAgent().
CTX-005.rationale: Different agents need different context: property-search=RAG-first, conversation-mgr=sliding window.

CTX-006.title: Build RAG context pipeline with score filtering
CTX-006.description: Hybrid retrieval, score threshold, source attribution, max results, token counting.
CTX-006.rationale: Score filtering prevents low-relevance dilution. Source attribution enables grounding compliance.

CTX-007.title: Add XML formatter for RAG context output
CTX-007.description: Wraps results in <context>/<source>/<content>/<relevance_score> tags for prompt integration.
CTX-007.rationale: XML creates clear source boundaries for model citation.

CROSS-001.title: Integrate orchestration with MCP tool layer
CROSS-001.description: Wire supervisor to execute via MCP ToolClient over stdio. Map MCP failures to AgentErrorType.TOOL_FAILURE.
CROSS-001.rationale: Orchestration and MCP are two halves. Without wiring, plans can't execute.

CROSS-002.title: Wire context engine into agent runtime
CROSS-002.description: Before LLM: assemble context via strategy, respect budget, include RAG, apply cache. After: update store/cache.
CROSS-002.rationale: Without integration, agents have raw messages and no grounding data.

CROSS-003.title: Add Docker env vars for orchestration config
CROSS-003.description: Env vars: AGENT_RUNTIME, MAX_BUDGET_USD, MAX_PLAN_STEPS, AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS, CB thresholds.
CROSS-003.rationale: Production needs env-based config without code changes.

CROSS-004.title: Update AGENTS.md with multi-agent coordination
CROSS-004.description: AGENT SWARM COORDINATION section: reservations, conflict zones, safe zones, bead assignment, communication, agent table.
CROSS-004.rationale: Without coordination rules, agents step on each other.

TEST-001.title: Write orchestration unit tests
TEST-001.description: Tests: registry CRUD, circuit breaker, error recovery, cost budget, DLQ, routing strategy, batch processor.
TEST-001.rationale: Most critical code path. Every request flows through orchestration.
TEST-001.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

TEST-002.title: Write context engine unit tests
TEST-002.description: Tests: token allocation, cache ops, sliding window, strategy budget compliance.
TEST-002.rationale: Context bugs cause overflow or stale data.
TEST-002.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

TEST-003.title: Write prompt system unit tests
TEST-003.description: Tests: Zod schema validation, cache layer building, grounding validator.
TEST-003.rationale: Schemas are contracts. Broken schemas let malformed data through.
TEST-003.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

TEST-004.title: Write observability unit tests
TEST-004.description: Tests: span creation/nesting, metric recording, cost per model, health aggregation.
TEST-004.rationale: Observability must be reliable - it diagnoses everything else.
TEST-004.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test

TEST-005.title: Write integration smoke tests for full pipeline
TEST-005.rationale: Smoke tests verify integration that unit tests cannot.
TEST-005.verification: cd agentic-ai && npm run build && npm run test
